Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WILLIAM MILLS, Esq. of Parker Shaffie LLP (Tel: 213-622-4441) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN B. SULLIVAN, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP (Tel: 415-397-2222) for defendant Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Parties inform the Court of their agreement. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified by the parties' agreement.

Motion to compel GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. SROG 86, 89, 92, 95 - denied. The further response is a straightforward yes. SROG 87, 90, 93, 96 - granted. Saying that discovery is continuing is improper. Interrogatory responses must be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available permits. CCP 2030.220. Any amendments to responses may be provided in response to a supplemental interrogatory. SROG 88, 91, 94, 97, 100 - denied. The interrogatory as written is overbroad. It calls not just for documents that may support an assertion, but those that relate or refer to any such documents without any intelligible limitation. Indeed, identifying what "relates to" a particular document usually requires counsel to engage in speculative analysis. SROG 98 - granted. This requests a simple yes/no. If a party cannot answer, it must say so. SROG 99 - granted. As with 87 and similar interrogatories, Plaintiff must supplement. SROG 101 - granted. The request is relevant to potential damages and so is discoverable. SROG 112, 115, 118 - granted. Even if it means copy/paste a large volume of text, it is permissible to discover what particular patent language is at issue. SROG 128 - granted. Citing one example tells the requesting party nothing about other "examples" and does not comply with the request. SROG 129 - denied. The answer that BBiTV does not have access to OpenTV's financial records is well taken; the answer further provides an estimate. This is sufficient to identify the limitations on ability to respond and to provide as complete an answer as possible. SROG 132, 135 - denied for the same reasons as 129. RFP 30, 32, 34, 41 - denied. A request for everything that "reflects, relates to, or refers to" is grossly overbroad and does not include an intelligible limitation. The examples that refer to production in other litigation underscore that there is no reasonably limiting factor that the Court can use to evaluate whether the production is sufficient. Revised responses due no later than March 31, 2023 per the parties' agreement.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.
